articles.title: Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
articles.summary: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps threatened to destroy OpenAI's $30 billion Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi if the US attacks Iranian power plants, publishing a video with satellite imagery of the facility.

articles.title: Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins wants data centers in space
articles.summary: Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins discusses the company's vision for expanding data centers into space. Cisco, a major networking infrastructure provider, aims to innovate beyond traditional ground-based data centers.

articles.title: Suno is a music copyright nightmare
articles.summary: Suno's AI music platform claims to prevent copyrighted material use, but its copyright filters are easily bypassed, allowing generation of near-identical imitations of popular songs.

articles.title: AI startup Rocket offers vibe McKinsey-style reports at a fraction of the cost
articles.summary: Rocket's AI platform generates McKinsey-style strategic reports at lower costs, expanding beyond code generation to include strategy, product building, and competitive intelligence.

articles.title: OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
articles.summary: Zero Shot, a venture capital fund founded by OpenAI alumni, is raising $100 million for its first fund and has already begun making investments.

articles.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
articles.summary: Google launched an offline AI dictation app using Gemma models to compete with apps like Whisper Flow, enabling voice-to-text without internet connectivity.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway secured $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The platform has attracted 2 million developers by addressing limitations of legacy cloud services for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200/month for AI coding assistance, sparking developer backlash. Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers identical functionality without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens. The startup decoded a coding challenge to recruit engineers, successfully hiring from thousands of applicants.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agent tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks due to its broad system access across apps and platforms. A recently fixed vulnerability highlights dangers of granting such extensive permissions.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: New Rowhammer attacks exploit memory vulnerabilities in Nvidia GPUs shared in cloud environments, allowing malicious users to gain full root control of host machines through repeated memory access patterns.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: New research shows quantum computers require far fewer resources than previously thought to break elliptic-curve encryption, with neutral atoms achieving 256-bit ECC cracking in 10 days using 100x less overhead.

articles.title: The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI
articles.summary: Silicon Valley leaders warn of AI-driven job displacement, with Anthropic researchers predicting recession and career disruption. CEO Dario Amodei claims AI could substitute human labor across all jobs within five years.

articles.title: AI is changing how small online sellers decide what to make
articles.summary: Small online sellers now use AI tools like Accio to streamline product sourcing and manufacturing decisions, replacing traditional time-consuming manual processes of contacting suppliers and factories.

articles.title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
articles.summary: SpaceX filed to launch orbital data centers to support AI without environmental impact on Earth. Tech companies like Amazon and Google explore space-based computing infrastructure.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, a frontier multimodal AI model designed for on-device processing. It combines advanced intelligence capabilities with efficient local execution.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer interaction technology, enabling new ways for AI systems to understand and interact with digital interfaces through advanced visual processing.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Falcon Perception represents advances in AI visual recognition and perception capabilities, enabling systems to better understand and process visual information from their environment.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its upcoming developments in this Microsoft blog conversation.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, providing developers with tools to build AI-powered agricultural solutions for sustainable and efficient farming practices.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google launches Flex and Priority inference tiers for Gemini API to balance cost and latency tradeoffs for developers.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 technologies, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect Brazil's forests. The tool aims to monitor and help preserve forest ecosystems.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence and performance.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live improves voice interactions with enhanced precision and lower latency, enabling more natural and fluid audio AI conversations.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches how AI can be manipulated to cause harm in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect people.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family while other major AI developments were discussed.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, a generative AI-powered graphics technology that enhances real-time photorealism in video games up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting conditions.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and ChatGPT's interactive math visuals. Topics include xAI updates, Anthropic lawsuit, and new AI research.

articles.title: As AI agents take on more tasks, governance becomes a priority
articles.summary: AI agents are increasingly autonomous, taking on planning and decision-making tasks. Organizations must implement governance frameworks with clear boundaries and controls to manage risks effectively.

articles.title: KiloClaw targets shadow AI with autonomous agent governance
articles.summary: KiloClaw is an enterprise platform addressing shadow AI by governing autonomous agents deployed by employees outside official channels, preventing data exposure and restoring organizational control.

articles.title: 5 best practices to secure AI systems
articles.summary: AI systems require multi-layered security strategies including data protection, access control, and monitoring. Key practices include enforcing strict access governance, encryption, and role-based permissions to limit exposure.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from Penn State and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving system reliability.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture enabling autonomous robots to navigate complex indoor environments by addressing localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering systems built around them emerge as the competitive advantage. The leak of Anthropic's Claude Code demonstrates that the harness engineering infrastructure is now the true differentiator.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to harvest credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 model advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming historical limitations in AI.

articles.title: Meta AI Releases EUPE: A Compact Vision Encoder Family Under 100M Parameters That Rivals Specialist Models Across Image Understanding, Dense Prediction, and VLM Tasks
articles.summary: Meta AI introduced EUPE, a compact vision encoder under 100M parameters that handles diverse vision tasks simultaneously while maintaining performance comparable to larger specialized models, enabling efficient AI deployment on edge devices.

articles.title: An Implementation Guide to Running NVIDIA Transformer Engine with Mixed Precision, FP8 Checks, Benchmarking, and Fallback Execution
articles.summary: This tutorial implements NVIDIA Transformer Engine with mixed-precision acceleration in Python, comparing baseline PyTorch versus Transformer Engine-enabled paths through benchmarking and performance analysis.

articles.title: RightNow AI Releases AutoKernel: An Open-Source Framework that Applies an Autonomous Agent Loop to GPU Kernel Optimization for Arbitrary PyTorch Models
articles.summary: RightNow AI released AutoKernel, an open-source framework using autonomous LLM agents to automatically optimize GPU kernels for PyTorch models, eliminating the need for specialized GPU expertise.

articles.title: LG G6 vs. Samsung S95H: I compared the best OLED TVs of 2026 and made a tough choice
articles.summary: Comparison of LG G6 and Samsung S95H premium OLED TVs. Both offer excellent picture and sound quality, but one emerges as the better purchase option.

articles.title: I found Android Auto's hidden shortcut that automates any task in your car - and it's brilliant
articles.summary: Android Auto has a hidden Custom Assistant feature that automates tasks in your car with minimal setup time, offering a brilliant hands-free solution for drivers.

articles.title: Samsung's latest TV firmware update fixes the Chromecast issue for older models - finally
articles.summary: Samsung's latest firmware update brings Google Cast support to older TV models dating back to 2024, fixing previous Chromecast compatibility issues.

articles.title: Top 5 Reranking Models to Improve RAG Results
articles.summary: Reranking models enhance RAG system results by improving retrieval quality. The article explores five top reranking models designed to optimize information retrieval and generation accuracy.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are evolving from backend dashboards to more prominent roles. Key trends to watch in 2026 include emerging applications and increased visibility of ML technologies.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: Agentic AI systems use state-managed interruptions to halt execution pipelines intentionally, creating human-in-the-loop approval gates for autonomous agents.

articles.title: The Geometry Behind the Dot Product: Unit Vectors, Projections, and Intuition
articles.summary: Article explores the geometric foundations of the dot product, explaining unit vectors, projections, and intuitive understanding of this fundamental mathematical concept.

articles.title: How to Run Claude Code Agents in Parallel
articles.summary: This article explains techniques for running Claude code agents in parallel to improve efficiency and performance when executing multiple tasks simultaneously.

articles.title: Behavior is the New Credential
articles.summary: Authentication is shifting from passwords and biometrics to behavioral analysis. Instead of what you know or look like, systems now verify identity through how you behave online.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights NVIDIA's breakthroughs in physical AI, showcasing robots transforming industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment from virtual training.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution across devices, from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, efficient models enable on-device agentic AI with real-time context processing.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield, a 3D real-time strategy expansion.

articles.title: Build AI-powered employee onboarding agents with Amazon Quick
articles.summary: Amazon Quick enables HR departments to build no-code AI agents that automate employee onboarding by answering questions, tracking compliance, and reducing manual tasks to accelerate new hire productivity.

articles.title: Accelerate agentic tool calling with serverless model customization in Amazon SageMaker AI
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI's serverless model customization uses reinforcement learning to improve agentic tool calling, reducing hallucinations and parameter errors without managing infrastructure.

articles.title: Building Intelligent Search with Amazon Bedrock and Amazon OpenSearch for hybrid RAG solutions
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock and OpenSearch enable intelligent search through hybrid RAG solutions, combining LLMs with real-time data retrieval for agentic AI assistants that handle complex tasks like hotel bookings.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA's batch mode VC-6 and Nsight tools accelerate vision AI pipelines by optimizing decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages to match model throughput improvements.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Gemma 4 launches as multimodal and multilingual models scaling from data centers to edge devices. Designed to meet growing demand for AI deployment across NVIDIA's infrastructure spectrum.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: AI inference achieves single-digit microsecond latency for algorithmic trading, enabling faster response times to market events. Advanced neural network models now compete with specialized hardware like FPGAs in capital markets.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study examines how different generations perceive social media advertising across platforms, exploring varying consumer attitudes toward ads based on age demographics.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough innovations in XR development.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to understand LLM behavior at scale through feature attribution and interpretability analysis, aiming to make AI systems more transparent and trustworthy.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: This article discusses information-driven design of imaging systems that use encoders to map objects to images while handling noise corruption. It presents an information estimator that quantifies measurement quality using only noisy data and noise models.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer paradigm instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks without TD learning's limitations.
